rankings.title: What 100 Years of American Finance Tells Us About Today
summary_bullets: Glass-Steagall separated commercial and investment banks.
System 1 (1933–1999) stable but not growth-optimized.
System 2 (1999–2008) deregulation led to leverage and GFC.
System 3 post-2010: private capital fills gap, factory model emerges.
Factory model: industrialization of fundraising and investing causes mismatches.
